mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate on a persistent basis, mechanically inflating the present value of future cash flows and producing a positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60-day returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals an inflection in labor market healing that is not yet priced by equity markets, triggering a positive repricing of forward earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE decline show negative or flat median 20-day forward returns, or the win rate for positive returns following the trigger is below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO and RSAFS indicates that production and final demand are expanding in tandem, a joint condition that reduces recession risk more powerfully than either series alone and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; when the two series diverge, the signal is void.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both increase month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-acceleration episodes fail to produce forward 20-day returns exceeding the unconditional sample mean, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) relative to either single-series model in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock housing recovery regime following a severe housing collapse, sustained month-over-month increases in HOUST signal renewed consumer confidence and construction-sector employment gains that propagate through the broader economy, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is expected to be weak or absent outside of post-shock recovery regimes.
failure_condition: HOUST momentum shows no significant positive correlation with forward 60-day SPX returns during the identified post-shock recovery window (2009-2012), or rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in PAYEMS combined with a simultaneous fall in UNRATE within the same month provides a confirmed labor market improvement signal that is more reliable than either metric alone, since PAYEMS gains without UNRATE declines can reflect labor force reentry rather than genuine tightening; this joint confirmation drives short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month while UNRATE simultaneously decreases
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down signal produces 5-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional returns (p > 0.10), or the interaction model underperforms a single-series PAYEMS-only baseline out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, markets begin pricing in an accelerated tightening cycle, raising discount rate uncertainty and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month until the inflation reading stabilizes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
regime_hint: low-rate regime with rising inflation risk
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce median 20-day forward returns that are flat or positive, or the negative-return hit rate in triggered periods is below 50% across a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, and provides an always-on leading signal of near-term corporate revenue strength that supports short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rolling correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 rank IC over any 2-year window, or the sign match rate is not better than a coin flip (50%) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle industrial recovery regime following a deep recession, sustained INDPRO growth signals restocking and capacity utilization gains that translate into corporate earnings upgrades, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; in late-cycle or contraction regimes the same INDPRO growth carries weaker or inverted predictive power due to overheating concerns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to show significantly positive forward 60-day return association during the 2009-2011 early-cycle window (p > 0.05), or the direction of the effect reverses when tested against a late-cycle comparison sample.
